Saturday, April 25, 2026
BREAKING NEWS
Punjab
April 25, 2026 12:48 PM
- Temperatures Soar Above Normal, Rain and Storms Likely from April 26
Punjab Newsline | Chandigarh
Punjab and Chandigarh are currently experiencing intense heatwave conditions, with temperatures rising significantly above normal levels.
Bathinda and Faridkot recorded a maximum temperature of 44.3°C, while Chandigarh registered 41.0°C. The temperature has increased by 0.5°C compared to Friday and is nearly five degrees above the seasonal average.
The Meteorological Centre in Chandigarh has issued a yellow alert for heatwave conditions across Punjab and Chandigarh for today. Warm night conditions are also expected in Chandigarh. However, relief may be on the way, as rain and thunderstorms have been forecast between April 26 and April 30.
Heatwave conditions are likely to prevail at isolated places in 10 districts Moga, Ferozepur, Faridkot, Fazilka, Barnala, Muktsar, Bathinda, Mansa, Sangrur, and Patiala.
Meanwhile, warm night conditions are expected at some locations in Amritsar, Tarn Taran, Ferozepur, Faridkot, Fazilka, Barnala, and Ludhiana. Additionally, light to moderate rainfall is likely at isolated places in Fazilka, Muktsar, and Bathinda.

title: Relentless Heatwave Continues Across Punjab and Chandigarh, Weather Change Expected Next Week - Rozana Spokesman
full_text:
Chandigarh also experienced intense heat, with temperatures hovering around 41°C, significantly above the seasonal average.
A prolonged spell of extreme heat has tightened its grip over Punjab and Chandigarh, with soaring temperatures and dry conditions affecting normal life across the region. Several districts are witnessing mercury levels well beyond 40°C, intensifying concerns over public health and safety.
Bathinda and Faridkot recorded the highest temperatures, both touching 44.3°C, making them the hottest locations in the state. Meanwhile, Chandigarh also experienced intense heat, with temperatures hovering around 41°C, significantly above the seasonal average.
Other major cities such as Amritsar, Ludhiana and Patiala reported similarly high readings, while hot winds during the day have worsened conditions in both urban and rural areas.
Meteorological officials have issued a yellow alert, warning that heatwave conditions will persist in districts including Mansa, Sangrur, Fazilka, Muktsar, and Ferozepur. Adding to the discomfort, night temperatures remain unusually high, preventing any significant cooling after sunset.
Residents have been advised to minimize outdoor activities during peak hours, stay hydrated, and take necessary precautions to avoid heat-related illnesses. Hospitals and health departments are also on alert to handle any emergencies arising from the severe weather.
However, a shift in weather patterns is expected soon. Experts indicate that an incoming western disturbance may bring rainfall, thunderstorms, and gusty winds to several parts of the state between April 26 and April 30. Southern districts are likely to witness the first spell of rain, followed by widespread changes across Punjab.
By April 28, the combined effect of cloud cover and precipitation is expected to reduce temperatures, offering partial relief from the ongoing heatwave. Until then, authorities continue to monitor the situation as the region endures one of the harshest heat spells of the season.
